title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Commissioner Delmon and Sir Henry Clithering discuss a letter regarding the Chipping Clegg Horn case sent by Jane Mapple, an observant woman staying at the Meddenham Royal Spa Hotel. Sir Henry suggests that Mapple has traveled to Meddingham specifically to assist with the murder investigation.

The investigators meet Miss Mapple, a kind, elderly woman and aunt of the author Raymond West, in Mr. Rolleston's private office. Miss Mapple presents findings regarding a young man who worked at the hotel counter and was previously believed to have directed a robbery before shooting himself. She reveals that the man actually forged a check, using a smudge of ink to alter a seven-pound amount to seventeen pounds. Miss Mapple, noting the discrepancy due to her thrifty nature, compares the man's deceptive gaze to a local fishmonger, Fred Taylor, and concludes he is a professional.

Furthermore, Miss Mapple observes that Rudy Schlesinger, who has a criminal record in Switzerland, is likely using a fake identity. She notes that Schlesinger frequently spends money on a red-haired waitress and suspects the waitress is withholding information out of fear. Finally, Miss Mapple posits that the young man did not act alone. She argues that while he might commit small thefts to fund a social life, his violent escalation into a hostage situation involving a revolver is inconsistent with his character, suggesting he was coerced by someone else. This observation resonates strongly with Inspector Corradino.
